behavior: An active insectivore that watches from exposed perches, makes short aerial sallies for flying prey, and also gleans caterpillars and other invertebrates from foliage; berries are taken occasionally. It nests in tree cavities and readily uses nest boxes.
field_marks: Small, compact, relatively large-headed flycatcher with an upright perching stance.
Short, blackish bill is broad at the base and sharply pointed; eye is large and dark.
Legs and feet are slender and slate gray.
Breeding male is black above and white below, with a complete white collar and bold white forehead patch.
Large white wing patch and pale-to-white rump contrast strongly with the dark wings.
Tail is fairly long and mostly dark; females and immature birds are gray-brown above but retain a prominent white wing patch.
habitat: Mature deciduous and other broad-leaved woodland with old, cavity-bearing trees; also wooded parks, gardens, orchards, and open woodland. Nonbreeding birds use broad-leaved woodland, including miombo.
